title: Selaginella denticulata
intro: Selaginella denticulata, also known as spike moss, is a type of evergreen, perennial plant belonging to the Selaginellaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella denticulata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Selaginella denticulata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Selaginella denticulata is a species of spikemoss, belonging to the genus Selaginella. This genus is part of the family Selaginellaceae and the order Selaginellales. The order Selaginellales is part of the subclass Lycopodiidae, which is a subgroup within the class Lycopodiopsida. Lycopodiopsida is a division of the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Selaginella denticulata reflects its position within the plant kingdom.
